name: Peak and Hermitage of Santo Domingo from Longas
name_original: Pico y Ermita de Santo Domingo desde Longás
region_intro: Hiking around Longás offers diverse terrain in the foothills of the Pyrenees, characterized by rolling hills, forested areas, and elevated peaks. The region features a network of trails that traverse varied landscapes, including historical paths and routes leading to prominent local landmarks. Hikers can expect ascents to viewpoints and descents through natural settings. The area provides opportunities for exploring both natural beauty and cultural sites. Best hiking trails around Longás The most popular hiking route is Puy Moné Loop from Pozo Pígalo, a 10.9 miles (17.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 23 minutes to complete, offering panoramic views from Puy Moné. Another top favourite among local hikers is Biel - GR 1 Historic Trail Loop, a moderate 8.1 miles (13.1 km) path. This route follows a section of the historic GR 1 trail, showcasing varied landscapes and cultural heritage. Local hikers also love the Peak and Hermitage of Santo Domingo from Longas, a 12.9 miles (20.7 km) trail leading through elevated terrain to a significant hermitage and peak, often completed in about 6 hours 29 minutes. Hiking around Longás is defined by its mix of challenging peak ascents, historic long-distance paths, and routes connecting local villages and hermitages. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels, catering to various hiking preferences. The routes in Longás are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from 8 reviews. More than 70 hikers have used komoot to explore Longás's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Longás?
A: There are 19 hiking trails around Longás, offering a diverse range of options for different skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult routes to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Longás?
A: Hiking around Longás offers diverse terrain in the foothills of the Pyrenees. You can expect rolling hills, forested areas, and elevated peaks. Trails often include ascents to viewpoints and descents through natural settings, traversing varied landscapes and historical paths.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Longás?
A: Yes, Longás offers 2 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed walk. These routes provide a gentle introduction to the region's natural beauty.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Longás?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers, Longás features 6 difficult trails. These often involve significant elevation changes and longer distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974270706/puy-mone-loop-from-pozo-pigalo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puy Moné Loop from Pozo Pígalo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 10.9 miles (17.6 km) with substantial ascents.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Longás area?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Longás are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974270706/puy-mone-loop-from-pozo-pigalo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puy Moné Loop from Pozo Pígalo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979973485/biel-gr-1-historic-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel - GR 1 Historic Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting places or landmarks can I see while hiking near Longás?
A: While hiking near Longás, you can explore various points of interest. Notable settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5258574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artieda&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5258524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrés&lt;/a&gt; are nearby. You might also encounter historical sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5271556" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or the significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979511378/peak-and-hermitage-of-santo-domingo-from-longas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santo Domingo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or viewpoints along the trails?
A: Yes, the region boasts several natural attractions and viewpoints. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2716013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pígalo Pools&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5816555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gypsum ravine&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic vistas from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5284678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puy Fonguera Summit and Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5286112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Ripas Altas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Longás?
A: The trails in Longás are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from 8 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied landscapes, from challenging peak ascents to historic paths, and the opportunities to connect with local villages and hermitages.

Q: Is Longás a dog-friendly hiking destination?
A: While specific dog regulations can vary by trail or protected area, generally, the natural environment around Longás is welcoming for dogs. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near livestock or in populated areas, and to check local signage for any restrictions.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Longás?
A: Yes, with 2 easy trails and 11 moderate trails, there are several options suitable for families. These routes offer less strenuous terrain and shorter distances, allowing families to enjoy the natural surroundings together. Always check the specific trail details for suitability with younger children.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Longás?
A: The best time for hiking in Longás is typically during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring snow to higher peaks.

Q: Are there any wild swimming spots near the hiking trails in Longás?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2716013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pígalo Pools&lt;/a&gt; are a notable natural attraction near Longás, offering a potential spot for wild swimming or cooling off during your hike. Always exercise caution and check local conditions before entering any natural water body.
tips: Beautiful views of the Pyrenees and Moncayo from the lonely Ermita de Santo Domingo
Located at 1524 meters of altitude, we are at the top of Santo Domingo located in the beautiful and extensive homonymous mountain range
